title: Goal Setting (Behavior)
description: Set or agree on a goal defined in terms of the behavior to be achieved

title: Goal Setting (Outcome)
description: Set or agree on a goal defined in terms of a positive outcome of wanted behavior

title: Problem Solving
description: Analyse or prompt the person to analyse factors influencing their behavior and generate or select strategies that include overcoming barriers

title: Action Planning
description: Prompt detailed planning of performance of the behavior including context, frequency, duration, or intensity

title: Review Behavior Goals
description: Review and reconsider previously set behavioral goals and modify if needed

title: Review Outcome Goals
description: Review and reconsider previously set outcome goals and modify if needed

title: Discrepancy Between Current Behavior and Goal
description: Draw attention to discrepancies between a person's current behavior and their previously set goals

title: Behavioral Contract
description: Create a written specification of the behavior to be performed, agreed by the person and witnessed by another

title: Commitment
description: Ask the person to affirm or reaffirm statements indicating commitment to change the behavior

title: Self-Monitoring of Behavior
description: Establish a method for the person to monitor and record their behavior as part of a behavior change strategy

title: Self-Monitoring of Outcome
description: Establish a method for the person to monitor and record the outcome of their behavior

title: Monitoring of Behavior by Others
description: Observe or record the person's behavior as part of a behavior change strategy

title: Monitoring of Outcome by Others
description: Observe or record outcomes of a person's behavior as part of a behavior change strategy

title: Feedback on Behavior
description: Provide informative or evaluative feedback on performance of the behavior

title: Feedback on Outcome
description: Provide feedback on the outcome of the person's behavior

title: Biofeedback
description: Provide feedback about the body using an external monitoring device

title: Social Support (Unspecified)
description: Advise on, arrange, or provide social support from friends, relatives, colleagues, or community

title: Social Support (Practical)
description: Advise on, arrange, or provide practical help for performance of the behavior

title: Social Support (Emotional)
description: Advise on, arrange, or provide emotional social support for performance of the behavior

title: Peer Support Groups
description: Create structured groups of people sharing similar challenges to provide mutual support, information exchange, and accountability for behavior change

title: Community Champions/Influencers
description: Identify, train, and support community members who naturally influence others' behaviors through their social position, credibility, or charisma

title: Mother-in-Law Engagement Program
description: Specifically target mothers-in-law as key behavior change agents given their influence over young women's health, nutrition, and reproductive decisions in South Asian families

title: Instruction on How to Perform Behavior
description: Advise or agree on how to perform the behavior, including skills training

title: Information About Antecedents
description: Provide information about antecedents that reliably predict performance of the behavior

title: Re-attribution
description: Elicit perceived causes of behavior and suggest alternative explanations

title: Behavioral Experiments
description: Advise on how to identify and test hypotheses about the behavior, its causes and consequences, by collecting and interpreting data

title: Information About Health Consequences
description: Provide information about health consequences of performing or not performing the behavior

title: Information About Social and Environmental Consequences
description: Provide information about social and environmental consequences of performing or not performing the behavior

title: Information About Emotional Consequences
description: Provide information about emotional consequences of performing or not performing the behavior

title: Salience of Consequences
description: Use methods to emphasize the consequences of performing or not performing the behavior

title: Anticipated Regret
description: Induce or raise awareness of expectations of future regret about performance or non-performance of the behavior

title: Information About Others' Approval
description: Provide information about what other people think about the behavior, particularly approval from valued others

title: Positive Deviance Approach
description: Identify and learn from community members who achieve better outcomes despite facing the same constraints as their peers

title: Demonstration of the Behavior
description: Provide an observable sample of the performance of the behavior for the person to aspire to or imitate

title: Social Comparison
description: Draw attention to others' performance to allow comparison with the person's own performance

title: Information About Others' Behavior
description: Provide information about what other people are doing, particularly people similar to the target individual

title: Prompts/Cues
description: Introduce or define environmental or social stimulus with the purpose of prompting or cueing the behavior

title: Cue Signalling Reward
description: Introduce or define a cue that signals the receipt of reward following performance of the behavior

title: Reduce Prompts/Cues
description: Withdraw gradually prompts or cues that have been used to prompt the behavior

title: Stimulus Control
description: Remove or alter stimuli that encourage undesirable behaviors and add stimuli that promote desired behaviors

title: Satiation
description: Advise the person to engage in the undesired behavior repeatedly until the urge diminishes

title: Habit Formation
description: Advise to perform the behavior in the same context repeatedly so it becomes automatic

title: Habit Reversal
description: Prompt rehearsal and repetition of an alternative behavior to replace an unwanted habitual behavior

title: Counter-Conditioning
description: Advise the person to associate the unwanted behavior with an unpleasant experience

title: Linking Behavior to Government Benefits
description: Create explicit connections between desired behaviors and eligibility for government scheme benefits to reinforce compliance

title: Social Norms Marketing
description: Use marketing approaches to shift perceptions of what is normal and socially acceptable, creating new positive norms through strategic communication

title: Behavioral Practice/Rehearsal
description: Prompt practice or rehearsal of the performance of the behavior one or more times in a context or at a time when not necessary

title: Behavioral Substitution
description: Prompt substitution of the unwanted behavior with a wanted or neutral behavior

title: Graded Tasks
description: Set easy-to-perform tasks, making them increasingly difficult but achievable, until the target behavior is performed

title: Overcorrection
description: Ask the person to practice the desired behavior beyond the point of proficiency

title: Generalization of Target Behavior
description: Advise the person to perform the wanted behavior in a variety of contexts

title: Behavior Stacking
description: Layer new behaviors onto existing routine behaviors to create chains of positive practices without requiring separate motivation for each

title: Credible Source
description: Present verbal or visual communication from a credible source in favor of or against the behavior

title: Pros and Cons
description: Advise the person to identify and compare reasons for wanting and not wanting to change the behavior

title: Comparative Imagining of Future Outcomes
description: Prompt the person to imagine and compare likely or possible outcomes of changed vs. unchanged behavior

title: Persuasive Communication
description: Use structured persuasive messaging designed to change beliefs, attitudes, and ultimately behaviors through logic, emotion, or credibility

title: Material Incentive (Behavior)
description: Inform that money, vouchers, or other valued objects will be delivered if the behavior is performed

title: Material Incentive (Outcome)
description: Inform that money, vouchers, or other valued objects will be delivered if the outcome is achieved

title: Social Reward
description: Arrange verbal or non-verbal reward if the behavior is performed, including expressions of approval and encouragement

title: Self-Reward
description: Prompt self-praise or self-reward if the behavior is performed

title: Non-specific Incentive
description: Inform that a reward will be delivered contingent on performance but the exact nature is not specified

title: Future Punishment
description: Inform that future punishment or unwanted consequences will result from performance of an unwanted behavior

title: Remove Aversive Stimulus
description: Advise that removal of an unpleasant consequence will occur if the wanted behavior is performed

title: Pharmacological Support
description: Provide or encourage the use of medications to facilitate behavior change

title: Reduce Negative Emotions
description: Advise on ways to reduce negative emotions that may be barriers to performing the behavior

title: Conserving Mental Resources
description: Advise on ways to minimize demands on mental resources to facilitate behavior change

title: Paradoxical Instructions
description: Advise the person to engage in an exaggerated form of the unwanted behavior

title: Motivational Interviewing Techniques
description: Use client-centered counseling approach that explores and resolves ambivalence about behavior change through empathetic, non-judgmental dialogue

title: Stress-Responsive Programming
description: Adapt behavior change messaging and expectations based on household stress levels, avoiding additional burden during crisis periods

title: Restructuring Physical Environment
description: Change or advise to change the physical environment in order to facilitate performance of the wanted behavior

title: Restructuring Social Environment
description: Change or advise to change the social environment to facilitate performance of the wanted behavior

title: Avoidance/Reducing Exposure to Cues for Unwanted Behavior
description: Advise on how to avoid exposure to specific social and contextual cues that prompt the unwanted behavior

title: Distraction
description: Advise or provide something to divert attention from the unwanted behavior

title: Adding Objects to the Environment
description: Add objects to the environment to facilitate performance of the behavior

title: Body Changes
description: Alter body structure, appearance, or functioning to facilitate behavior change

title: Identification of Self as Role Model
description: Inform the person that their behavior may be an example for others to follow

title: Framing/Reframing
description: Suggest the deliberate adoption of a perspective or new perspective on behavior to change cognitions or emotions about it

title: Incompatible Beliefs
description: Draw attention to discrepancies between current or past behavior and self-image to create discomfort that motivates behavior change

title: Valued Self-Identity
description: Advise the person to write or complete ratings of valued self-identities to affirm their sense of self

title: Cognitive Dissonance
description: Draw attention to inconsistencies between a person's attitudes and their actual behavior to create discomfort and motivate change

title: Narrative Identity Shift
description: Help individuals and communities construct new narratives about who they are that incorporate the desired behavior as a core part of their identity

title: Aspirational Identity Construction
description: Help individuals envision and adopt an aspirational identity that naturally incorporates the desired behavior as part of who they want to become

title: Behavior Cost
description: Arrange for withdrawal of something valued if an unwanted behavior is performed

title: Reward Approximation
description: Reward any behavior that approximates the target behavior, gradually requiring closer approximation for reward

title: Situation-Specific Reward
description: Reward the behavior in one situation but not in another

title: Reward Completion
description: Build in a reward for completion of the behavior

title: Verbal Persuasion About Capability
description: Tell the person that they can successfully perform the behavior, arguing against self-doubts

title: Mental Rehearsal of Successful Performance
description: Advise the person to rehearse the successful performance of the behavior mentally

title: Focus on Past Success
description: Advise the person to think about or list previous successes in performing the behavior or similar behaviors

title: Self-Talk
description: Prompt positive self-talk before and during the behavior

title: Mastery Experience
description: Provide opportunities for the person to successfully perform the behavior or a simpler version of it, building confidence through direct experience of success

title: Imaginary Punishment
description: Advise the person to imagine performing the unwanted behavior and experiencing punishment

title: Imaginary Reward
description: Advise the person to imagine performing the wanted behavior in a context where they would gain reward

title: Vicarious Consequences
description: Prompt observation of the consequences (reward or punishment) of others' behavior to influence behavior change

title: Participatory Learning and Action (PLA) Cycles
description: Facilitate community groups through structured cycles of identifying problems, planning, acting, and reflecting on health and social issues

title: Community Scorecard
description: Enable communities to rate and score the quality and accessibility of local services, creating accountability pressure for improvement

title: Social Audit
description: Facilitate systematic public examination of government expenditure and service delivery by community members

title: Gram Sabha Activation
description: Strengthen village assembly meetings as forums for collective decision-making on behavior change priorities and resource allocation

title: Self-Help Group (SHG) Leveraging
description: Use existing SHG platforms and networks to deliver behavior change messaging, create peer support, and establish social norms

title: Community-Led Total Sanitation (CLTS) Triggering
description: Use facilitated community analysis of open defecation practices to trigger collective disgust and motivate community-wide toilet adoption

title: Jan Sunwai (Public Hearing)
description: Organize public hearings where community members present grievances and evidence directly to government officials for accountability

title: Collective Efficacy Building
description: Build a community's shared belief that they can work together to achieve change, through shared successes and joint action

title: Inter-Community Exposure Visits
description: Organize visits for community members to observe and learn from successful behavior change in comparable communities

title: Community Radio for Behavior Change
description: Use community-owned radio stations to broadcast locally relevant behavior change content, discussions, and community voices

title: Folk Media and Street Theatre (Nukkad Natak)
description: Use traditional performance arts - street theatre, puppet shows, folk songs, and drama - to communicate behavior change messages in culturally resonant formats

title: Women's Collective Action
description: Facilitate organized women's groups taking collective action to address behavior change barriers and enforce community norms

title: Participatory Rural Appraisal (PRA)
description: Use visual, participatory tools like social mapping, wealth ranking, seasonal calendars, and transect walks to enable communities to analyze their own situation and plan behavior changes

title: Youth-Led Campaigns
description: Engage young people as designers and leaders of behavior change campaigns targeting their peers and communities

title: Convergent Action Planning
description: Bring together multiple government departments and community stakeholders to create coordinated behavior change plans that address interconnected issues

title: Faith-Based Organization Partnerships
description: Partner with temples, mosques, churches, gurdwaras, and faith-based organizations to deliver behavior change messaging through trusted religious networks

title: Storytelling and Narrative Persuasion (Katha)
description: Use stories, parables, and narrative formats to convey behavior change messages in culturally resonant ways

title: Religious Leader Engagement
description: Partner with religious leaders and institutions to endorse and promote behavior change through religious teachings and community trust

title: Festival-Linked Campaigns
description: Time and theme behavior change campaigns to align with major festivals, leveraging heightened community engagement and cultural resonance

title: Local Proverb and Idiom Integration
description: Embed behavior change messages within local proverbs, sayings, and idiomatic expressions that carry cultural authority

title: Cultural Broker Engagement
description: Identify and engage individuals who bridge traditional and modern knowledge systems to facilitate culturally appropriate behavior change

title: Life-Course Event Messaging
description: Link behavior change communication to key life events (pregnancy, birth, marriage, harvest) when families are naturally receptive to new information

title: Seasonal Calendar Alignment
description: Align behavior change interventions with agricultural and seasonal cycles that govern daily life in rural communities

title: Indigenous Knowledge Integration
description: Incorporate traditional and indigenous knowledge systems into behavior change programs, validating local wisdom while introducing complementary evidence-based practices

title: Edutainment and Serial Drama
description: Use entertainment media - TV/radio serial dramas, films, and web series - to model behavior change through character development and storylines

title: Wall Art and Murals for BCC
description: Use painted walls, murals, and public art in villages and urban spaces to communicate behavior change messages visually and continuously

title: Leveraging Kinship and Caste Networks
description: Use existing kinship, gotra, biradari, and caste-based networks as channels for behavior change communication and norm diffusion

title: Gender Dialogue Sessions
description: Facilitate structured conversations between men and women to examine and challenge gender roles, power dynamics, and norms that affect behavior

title: Engaging Men and Boys
description: Design specific interventions targeting men and boys to examine masculinity norms and become allies in gender equity and behavior change

title: Addressing Son Preference and Sex Selection
description: Challenge cultural norms favoring sons through awareness, positive valuation of daughters, and enforcement of PCPNDT Act

title: Menstrual Health Normalization
description: Challenge taboos and stigma around menstruation through education, product access, and open conversation to support girls' and women's dignity and participation

title: Women's Economic Empowerment as Behavior Change Lever
description: Build women's economic agency through skill building, market access, and financial control to enable broader behavior changes in health, education, and nutrition

title: Safe Space Creation for Adolescent Girls
description: Establish dedicated physical and programmatic spaces where adolescent girls can learn, discuss, and build agency away from restrictive social surveillance

title: Addressing Dowry and Child Marriage Norms
description: Challenge economic and social norms driving dowry and early marriage through community dialogue, legal awareness, and alternative ceremonies

title: Shared Household Labor Negotiation
description: Facilitate conversations and agreements between household members to redistribute unpaid domestic and care work more equitably

title: Masculinity Reflection Circles
description: Create structured spaces for men to reflect on how rigid masculinity norms affect their own health, relationships, and wellbeing

title: GBV Response and Prevention through BCC
description: Address gender-based violence through community awareness, survivor support, bystander intervention training, and norm change

title: Addressing GBV Through Community Norms
description: Transform community tolerance of gender-based violence through collective norm-setting, survivor support, and accountable community systems

title: IVR-Based Voice Reminders
description: Use Interactive Voice Response systems to deliver automated voice-based behavior change messages and reminders in local languages to basic mobile phones

title: SMS and WhatsApp Nudges
description: Send targeted text or WhatsApp messages to prompt, remind, and reinforce behavior change at optimal timing

title: Video-Based Modeling via Mobile
description: Use short videos on smartphones showing peers performing desired behaviors, shared through WhatsApp or community viewing

title: mHealth Applications for Frontline Workers
description: Equip frontline health workers with mobile applications that guide counseling, track behaviors, and provide decision support at the point of care

title: Digital Payment Incentives
description: Use digital payment systems (UPI, DBT) to deliver instant financial incentives for behavior performance, leveraging speed and transparency

title: Community WhatsApp for Social Accountability
description: Create and moderate community WhatsApp groups where citizens can report service delivery issues, share evidence, and demand accountability from duty bearers

title: Telemedicine-Linked Behavior Support
description: Use telemedicine consultations to provide personalized behavior change counseling and follow-up from specialist providers in remote areas

title: AI Chatbot Counseling
description: Use AI-powered chatbots to provide personalized, accessible health and behavior change counseling through messaging platforms

title: Digital Health Records as Feedback
description: Use digitized health records to provide patients and families with visual feedback on health trajectories and behavior adherence

title: Voice Message Campaigns in Local Languages
description: Deliver behavior change messages through pre-recorded voice messages in local languages and dialects to overcome literacy barriers

title: Default Option Setting
description: Set the desired behavior as the default choice that requires no action, while making the undesired behavior require active opt-out

title: Simplification of Complex Choices
description: Reduce the cognitive complexity of decision-making by simplifying options, forms, processes, and information

title: Commitment Devices
description: Create mechanisms that bind a person to their future intentions, making it costly or difficult to deviate from planned behavior

title: Friction Reduction
description: Remove small barriers, delays, and hassles that disproportionately prevent people from performing desired behaviors

title: Social Proof Displays
description: Make the prevalence of desired behavior visible in the physical environment to establish it as the social norm

title: Anchoring and Reference Points
description: Provide a reference number or standard that influences subsequent judgments and decisions about behavior

title: Framing Effects
description: Present the same information in different frames (gain vs. loss, frequency vs. percentage) to influence how it is perceived and acted upon

title: Temptation Bundling
description: Pair an unpleasant or tedious behavior with an enjoyable activity to increase the likelihood of performing the desired behavior

title: Loss Aversion Leveraging
description: Frame behavior change in terms of what people stand to lose by not acting, leveraging the psychological tendency to weigh losses more heavily than equivalent gains

title: Micro-Incentives
description: Provide very small but immediate rewards for specific behaviors, leveraging the disproportionate motivational impact of small certain rewards over larger uncertain ones

title: Pre-Commitment Strategies
description: Ask people to commit to a future behavior at a time when their motivation is high and barriers seem manageable

title: Implementation Intentions (If-Then Plans)
description: Help people form specific if-then plans linking situational cues to behavioral responses, automating decision-making

title: Choice Overload Reduction
description: Reduce the number of options presented to people to prevent decision paralysis and increase the likelihood of action

title: Sunk Cost Highlighting
description: Remind people of investments already made in a behavior change process to motivate continuation rather than abandonment

title: Salience and Attention Capture
description: Make the desired behavior or its benefits highly noticeable and attention-grabbing in the decision environment through visual design, color, size, or novelty

title: WASH Behavior Change Package
description: Integrated approach combining handwashing promotion, safe water treatment, sanitation use, and menstrual hygiene through multi-channel BCC

title: Immunization Promotion and Demand Generation
description: Multi-pronged approach to increase vaccine uptake through awareness, trust-building, reminder systems, and addressing vaccine hesitancy

title: First 1000 Days Nutrition Counseling
description: Stage-specific nutrition behavior change from conception through the child's second birthday, targeting feeding, dietary diversity, and care practices

title: TB Treatment Adherence Support
description: Behavioral support systems ensuring TB patients complete their full treatment course, combining monitoring, incentives, and peer support

title: Family Planning Counseling (Informed Choice)
description: Provide comprehensive, non-coercive counseling on the full range of contraceptive methods, supporting autonomous and informed decision-making

title: ORS-Zinc Promotion for Diarrhea Management
description: Promote home-based oral rehydration and zinc supplementation as first-line treatment for childhood diarrhea, replacing inappropriate antibiotic use

title: Clean Cooking Fuel Adoption
description: Promote transition from solid biomass fuels to LPG or other clean cooking fuels through awareness of health impacts, subsidy access, and supply chain reliability

title: Maternal Health Continuum of Care
description: Ensure behavior change messaging covers the full maternal health continuum: antenatal care, institutional delivery, postnatal care, and newborn care

title: Breastfeeding Promotion and Support
description: Multi-level support for early and exclusive breastfeeding through counseling, family engagement, and workplace/community enabling environments

title: Anemia Reduction Through Behavioral Approaches
description: Promote iron-rich dietary practices, IFA supplementation adherence, and deworming uptake through multi-pronged BCC

title: NCD Risk Factor Management
description: Address behavioral risk factors for non-communicable diseases through screening, lifestyle counseling, and self-management support

title: Smoke-Free and Tobacco-Free Environments
description: Create tobacco-free zones in public spaces, schools, and workplaces to reduce exposure, denormalize tobacco use, and support cessation

title: Kangaroo Mother Care Promotion
description: Promote skin-to-skin contact between mother and newborn for preterm/low birth weight babies through training, family counseling, and facility-based initiation

title: Financial Literacy Campaigns
description: Build knowledge and skills for managing money, savings, credit, and financial products through structured learning programs

title: Crop Diversification Adoption
description: Promote shift from monocropping to diversified cropping systems through demonstration, economic evidence, and market linkage

title: Insurance Uptake Promotion
description: Increase enrollment in crop, health, and life insurance through simplified products, trust-building, and claims facilitation

title: Enterprise Behavior Development
description: Build entrepreneurial mindset and behaviors in SHG members and rural youth through structured enterprise development programs

title: Migration Decision Support
description: Provide information and support for safe, informed migration decisions rather than discouraging migration, addressing both sending and receiving contexts

title: Savings Group Formation and Maintenance
description: Facilitate formation and sustained operation of community savings groups as platforms for financial behavior change and mutual support

title: Cooperative and FPO Formation
description: Build collective economic action through farmer producer organizations and cooperatives that enable better market access and bargaining power

title: Market Linkage Behaviors
description: Build farmer and producer behaviors for successful market participation including quality standards, aggregation, negotiation, and digital market access

title: Parental Engagement in Learning
description: Build parents' capacity and motivation to support children's learning at home through structured guidance and materials

title: School Enrollment and Retention Campaigns
description: Community-level campaigns promoting school enrollment, regular attendance, and retention through awareness, incentives, and barrier removal

title: Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) Habits
description: Build daily reading and math practice habits in children through structured activities, games, and family involvement

title: Adolescent Life Skills Education
description: Build critical thinking, decision-making, communication, and socio-emotional skills in adolescents through structured curricula

title: School Management Committee Activation
description: Strengthen school governance by building capacity of parent-community committees to monitor school quality and demand accountability

title: Anti-Child Labor and Child Protection
description: Address child labor through awareness of laws, alternative livelihood support for families, and school enrollment drives

title: Early Childhood Stimulation
description: Promote responsive caregiving and early stimulation behaviors that support cognitive, language, and socio-emotional development in children under 3

title: Digital Learning for Rural Youth
description: Provide digital literacy and online learning opportunities for rural youth to build skills, access information, and participate in the digital economy

title: Climate-Adaptive Farming Practices
description: Promote adoption of farming practices that build resilience to climate variability including drought, flood, and temperature extremes

title: Waste Segregation and Management
description: Promote household-level waste segregation and responsible disposal through awareness, infrastructure provision, and community systems

title: Water Conservation Behaviors
description: Promote water-saving practices in agriculture and household use through awareness, technology, and community water management

title: Renewable Energy Uptake
description: Promote adoption of solar, biogas, and other renewable energy sources through awareness, financing, and demonstration

title: Disaster Preparedness Behaviors
description: Build community and household preparedness behaviors for floods, cyclones, earthquakes, and other climate-related disasters

title: Biodiversity Conservation Behaviors
description: Promote community-led biodiversity conservation through awareness of ecological connections, traditional conservation practices, and sustainable use

title: Crop Residue Management
description: Promote alternatives to crop residue burning through awareness of health/environmental impacts and subsidized access to management technologies

title: Natural Resource Management Behaviors
description: Promote sustainable use of common pool resources (forests, water, grazing lands) through community-based governance and conservation practices

title: RTI Usage Promotion
description: Build community capacity to use the Right to Information Act as a tool for accessing information and ensuring government accountability

title: Government Scheme Awareness and Access
description: Systematically inform communities about available government schemes and support enrollment through facilitation and simplification

title: Voter Awareness and Political Participation
description: Promote informed voter participation through awareness of electoral rights, candidate platforms, and democratic processes

title: Panchayat Participation and Local Governance
description: Build citizen capacity to participate in local governance through Gram Sabha attendance, engagement with elected representatives, and community monitoring

title: MGNREGA Rights Awareness
description: Build awareness of MGNREGA entitlements including 100 days guaranteed work, minimum wages, worksite facilities, and unemployment allowance

title: PDS Entitlement Awareness
description: Ensure beneficiary awareness of Public Distribution System entitlements, quality standards, and complaint mechanisms

title: Citizen Feedback and Grievance Mechanisms
description: Strengthen citizen capacity to provide feedback on services and file grievances through accessible channels

title: Budget Literacy and Tracking
description: Build community capacity to understand, track, and influence local government budget allocations and expenditures

title: Citizen Report Cards
description: Systematically collect citizen feedback on public services through surveys and present findings to service providers and policy makers for accountability

title: MoSPI eSankhyiki MCP
description: India's National Statistics Office MCP server — access PLFS, CPI, IIP, NAS, WPI, and Environmental Statistics programmatically.

title: Census of India API
description: Developer API for 2011 and 2001 Census published tables — demographic, housing, and population data.

title: NFHS-5 Survey Data
description: National Family Health Survey covering 707 districts, 636K households — health, nutrition, maternal and child indicators.

title: NITI Aayog NDAP
description: National Data Analytics Platform — cross-sectoral datasets standardized to common schema for merging and analysis.

title: RBI Data Portal
description: Reserve Bank of India economic, banking, monetary policy, and financial statistics.

title: data.gov.in
description: Open Government Data Platform India — 10,000+ datasets across all central and state ministries.

title: ASER Education Data
description: Annual Status of Education Report — 650,000+ children surveyed, rural learning outcomes across 26 states.

title: NSSO Survey Data
description: National Sample Survey micro-data on consumption expenditure, employment, enterprise, and social conditions.

title: India Meteorological Department
description: Weather observations, climate data, monsoon forecasts, and cyclone tracking for India.

title: UDISE+ Education Data
description: Unified District Information System for Education — school-level enrollment, infrastructure, and teacher data.

title: NCRB Crime Statistics
description: National Crime Records Bureau — crime, accident, prison, and juvenile justice statistics.

title: PMGSY Road Infrastructure
description: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana — rural road connectivity, habitation coverage data.

title: Tribal Affairs Data Portal
description: Statistics on tribal populations, welfare schemes, and development indicators.

title: Women & Child Development Data
description: ICDS, nutrition, maternal health, Beti Bachao, and child welfare statistics.

title: World Bank Data MCP
description: Query 1,000+ development indicators for 200+ countries — poverty, health, education, economics.

title: IMF Data MCP Server
description: International Monetary Fund economic data and time series via SDMX APIs.

title: UNDP Human Development Data
description: Human Development Index API — composite indices and time-series data from 1990 to 2023.

title: UNICEF Data Warehouse
description: 900+ child welfare indicators across 247 countries via SDMX REST API.

title: UNICEF MCP Server
description: Easy-read conversion, translation tools, and accessibility features for development content.

title: OECD Data Explorer
description: Economic, social, and environmental statistics for OECD member and partner countries.

title: UN Population Prospects
description: World population projections, mortality, fertility, and migration estimates to 2100.

title: Multidimensional Poverty Index
description: OPHI/UNDP poverty measurement beyond income — health, education, living standards across 100+ countries.

title: World Inequality Database
description: Income and wealth inequality data across countries and historical time periods.

title: Humanitarian Data Exchange
description: OCHA humanitarian datasets — crises, displacement, needs assessments, response data.

title: Open SDG Data Hub
description: All 17 Sustainable Development Goals indicator tracking and progress monitoring.

title: UNHCR Refugee Statistics
description: Refugee, asylum-seeker, internally displaced, and stateless population data.

title: UN Women Data Hub
description: Gender equality statistics, women's empowerment indicators worldwide.

title: Global Burden of Disease
description: Comprehensive health loss data — mortality, morbidity, disability, and risk factors globally.

title: WHO Global Health Observatory
description: 1,000+ health indicators for 194 member states via OData API.

title: DHS Program API
description: 470+ demographic and health surveys from 90+ countries — reproductive health, nutrition, mortality.

title: Medical MCP Server
description: Integrated access to FDA drug data, WHO guidelines, PubMed research, and RxNorm.

title: Healthcare Data MCP
description: Government health datasets — spending, provider networks, drug pricing, quality metrics.

title: GAVI Vaccine Data
description: Immunization coverage, equity gaps, and vaccine alliance program statistics.

title: Malaria Atlas Project
description: Spatial malaria prevalence, incidence, mortality, and intervention coverage maps.

title: NCD Risk Factor Collaboration
description: Non-communicable disease risk factor trends — diabetes, obesity, hypertension, cholesterol.

title: HealthMap
description: Real-time disease surveillance, outbreak detection, and epidemic intelligence.

title: Global Nutrition Report
description: Nutrition indicators — stunting, wasting, overweight, micronutrient deficiency globally.

title: PEPFAR HIV/AIDS Data
description: US President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief — treatment, prevention, care data.

title: TB DIAH Indicators
description: Tuberculosis surveillance, diagnosis, treatment outcomes, and program performance.

title: Our World in Data Health
description: COVID-19 and global health datasets with interactive visualizations and downloads.

title: WHO Data Portal
description: Unified WHO data access — indicators, metadata, country profiles, and comparisons.

title: OpenDisease
description: Open-source infectious disease modeling, forecasting, and epidemiological data.

title: UNESCO DataHub
description: Education statistics and SDG 4 indicators — 808,000+ data points across countries.

title: UNESCO UIS Data Browser
description: Education, science, culture, and communication statistics with bulk download.

title: Education Data MCP
description: Urban Institute API — schools, districts, universities, enrollment, and finance data.

title: EduBase MCP
description: E-learning platform integration — quiz systems, STEM support, automatic grading.

title: World Bank EdStats
description: Comprehensive education statistics — enrollment, completion, literacy, expenditure.

title: GEM Report Data
description: Global Education Monitoring Report — equity, quality, and inclusion datasets.

title: Learning Poverty Data
description: World Bank/UNESCO measure of children who cannot read by age 10.

title: PISA Results Database
